provenance.source_corpus: Natural revegetation of two block-cut mined peatlands in eastern Canada

source_spans.text: Complete Sphagnum re-establishment on the lower, lateral parts of the trenches reveals that topography and high water table have more impact on the development of a Sphagnum cover than time since abandonment of exploitation.

Complete Sphagnum re-establishment on the lower, lateral parts of the trenches reveals that topography and high water table have more impact on the development of a Sphagnum cover than time since abandonment of exploitation.

nodes.name: Establishment of small cushions of Sphagnum

source_spans.text: Establishment of small cushions of Sphagnum can initiate formation of new fibric peat accumulation.

nodes.name: formation of new fibric peat accumulation

source_spans.text: Establishment of small cushions of Sphagnum can initiate formation of new fibric peat accumulation.
A sufficient expansion of Sphagnum cushions under ericaceous shrubs led to a complete overgrowth of Eriophorum spissum tussocks and partial overgrowth of Polytrichum strictum mats.

nodes.name: complete overgrowth of Eriophorum spissum tussocks

source_spans.text: A sufficient expansion of Sphagnum cushions under ericaceous shrubs led to a complete overgrowth of Eriophorum spissum tussocks and partial overgrowth of Polytrichum strictum mats.

nodes.name: partial overgrowth of Polytrichum strictum mats

source_spans.text: A sufficient expansion of Sphagnum cushions under ericaceous shrubs led to a complete overgrowth of Eriophorum spissum tussocks and partial overgrowth of Polytrichum strictum mats.
Thus, the high water table at both sites may also be attributed to the progressive reconstitution of the acrotelm.
Thus, the high water table at both sites may also be attributed to the progressive reconstitution of the acrotelm.
Sufficient rewetting by natural degradation of the drainage system may have limited decay, oxidation, and mineralization of the residual peat.

nodes.name: decay, oxidation, and mineralization of the residual peat

source_spans.text: Sufficient rewetting by natural degradation of the drainage system may have limited decay, oxidation, and mineralization of the residual peat.
The physical properties of residual peat influence its capacity to keep water in reserve.

nodes.name: capacity to keep water in reserve

source_spans.text: The physical properties of residual peat influence its capacity to keep water in reserve.
